A Memoir by Anne Corwin

In the Wake
of Amen

A Barefoot Journey Into Faith

What if following Christ meant letting go of everything—home, comfort, logic—and stepping into the unknown with nothing but a robe, a bedroll, and the belief that God would provide?

Get the Book on Amazon Follow Anne's Journey
★★★★★

Verified Amazon Reviews

The Book

A Journey of Radical
Faith & Surrender

In this deeply personal and thought-provoking memoir, journalist and social worker Anne Corwin invites you into the seven-year journey she spent walking in the robe. Inspired by a group of Christ-followers who believed that Lightning Amen was a manifestation of Christ—not as a sacrifice for sin this time, but as a guide through modern chaos.

"With raw honesty and deep conviction, Anne wrestles with ego, doubt, devotion, and the tension between earthly logic and spiritual surrender."

Her journey is one of transformation—both internal and external—as she walks barefoot on holy ground, plants seeds of Eden in unlikely places, and follows a call far beyond comfort zones or convention.

Whether you agree with her beliefs or simply seek a deeper understanding of what it means to follow Christ wholeheartedly, In the Wake of Amen will challenge you to look at your own faith through a new lens.

This book is for you if you love…

  • Faith-based memoirs
  • Christian nonconformist stories
  • Spiritual transformation journeys
  • Raw, honest accounts of following Christ
  • Radical discipleship and surrendered living

"Could her commitment stir your own? Could her faith inspire yours? That's the question—and only your heart can answer."

Buy on Amazon

About the Author

Anne Corwin

Anne Corwin was born in Colombia to pioneer missionary parents and has lived in the USA for most of her life, dividing her career between journalism and social work. She obtained a master's degree in social work and worked primarily in medical settings.

Her journalistic experience included producing The Servant Leader—a "good news" supplement to a local newspaper in Uvalde, Texas—along with years as an investigative reporter, political and lifestyle columnist, and feature writer for daily and weekly newspapers in Atlanta and Tallahassee.

She currently resides in southern California, praying that everyone will fall in love—with God first, and then with one another.

Journalist & Writer Master's in Social Work Investigative Reporter Missionary Heritage Faith Memoir Author Social Worker Gardner Christ Family

Podcast Guests

Book Anne as a Podcast Guest

Anne is a compelling, warm, and deeply thoughtful guest—a journalist who asks hard questions, a social worker who leads with compassion, and a woman who lived every word she writes.

🎙️

Unique Life Story

Born on the mission field in Colombia, Anne brings a cross-cultural lens to faith, obedience, and following Christ in modern America.

📰

Journalist's Clarity

With years as an investigative reporter, Anne communicates complex spiritual ideas with precision, warmth, and zero jargon.

🕊️

Seven Years Inside a Radical Pilgrim Community

Anne lived it—no second-hand accounts. She speaks to radical discipleship, doubt, surrender, and the war between logic and belief.

💙

Social Worker's Heart

Her background brings depth to conversations about nonviolence, community care, and the intersection of faith and human wellbeing.

Topics Anne Speaks On

Radical faith & obedience What it truly means to follow Christ Leaving comfort for conviction Nonviolence as a spiritual practice Doubt, ego & spiritual surrender Community & modern discipleship Faith in unconventional spaces From journalism to spiritual memoir Living in complete dependence on God Bridging social work & faith
